artPrompt: A terrified figure stands small at the bottom of the wide frame, hands raised, as a colossal shadowed auditor looms above behind a desk stacked with towering blank documents, pointing one accusing finger down into a single harsh cone of light. Scattered dice and coins lie exposed on the desk. Oppressive arcane glow seeps from the files, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: The Audit from Hell
text: Target opponent reveals their hand and their library's top five cards. For each die roll or coin flip they've made this game (count them honestly — the auditor will know), that player discards a card. Then they reorganize their remaining cards by mana value, ascending, while you watch.
flavorText: "This receipt is from a roll you made on turn two. Explain it."
